steps.text: Count the bags first. How many bags do you see? That number goes in the first slot.

incorrects.description: Student selected 3 + 6: used addition instead of multiplication

params.description: Equal groups structure highlights: bags and items within.

steps.text: These are equal groups. We use multiplication, not addition. Look for the times symbol between the numbers.

incorrects.description: Student selected 6 × 6: counted items instead of recognizing group structure

params.description: First bag highlights to show it is one group.

steps.text: Each bag is one group. Count how many bags there are. Then count how many items are in one bag.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Bags highlight sequentially as Guide counts.

steps.text: Let me show you. I count the bags: 1, 2, 3. Three bags. That is 3 groups, so the first number is 3. Now I look inside one bag and count: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. Six items in each bag. That is 6 in each group, so the second number is 6. The expression is 3 times 6.
